skill.name: quetrex-architect
skill.description: Guide Quetrex feature work with TDD, strict TypeScript, Next.js App Router patterns, ShadCN UI, Zod validation, and secure coding practices.
skill.summary: Enforces Quetrex architecture, testing, UI, validation, and security patterns for feature work.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, environment access, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are Markdown TypeScript examples and instructional anti-patterns, not executable skill code or malicious behavior. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network activity, or real secret handling was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur in Markdown fenced TypeScript examples and one SQL string anti-pattern. The skill only allows Read, Grep, and Glob tools, so these lines are examples rather than executable Ruby or shell backticks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations align with Markdown code fence delimiters or illustrative TypeScript strings. No shell command, Ruby execution context, or tool permission for command execution is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment Variable Access Used as Safe Guidance
low_findings.description: The environment variable references show how to avoid hardcoded secrets. They reference OPENAI_API_KEY as a placeholder and do not read, print, store, or transmit a real secret.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code is inside an instructional security section and is explicitly marked as the recommended pattern. No network call or data exfiltration path accompanies the environment variable reference.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Keyword Matches Without Dangerous Behavior
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections map to description text and validation examples. The reviewed lines do not implement cryptography, system discovery, or host inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are metadata or ordinary TypeScript validation examples. There is no API use for crypto primitives, system commands, file enumeration, or network reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Quetrex feature work can drift from required TypeScript, testing, UI, and security patterns. This skill guides implementation and review against the project architecture rules.
actual_capabilities: Checks Quetrex feature plans against TypeScript strict mode expectations.
Guides Next.js App Router choices for server and client components.
Promotes API input validation with Zod schemas.
Reviews ShadCN UI usage and React Hook Form patterns.
Applies TDD steps and coverage expectations to feature work.
Identifies common security mistakes such as hardcoded secrets and unsafe queries.
limitations: It is written for Quetrex projects and may not fit other codebases.
It provides guidance only and cannot run tests with its allowed tools.
It references ShadCN UI standards current to November 2025.
It depends on project files such as CLAUDE.md and pattern memory when available.

use_cases.title: Build a Quetrex Feature
use_cases.description: Use the skill to plan a feature with tests, validation, UI components, and App Router boundaries before implementation.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Use the skill to find architecture violations, missing validation, weak typing, and inconsistent UI patterns in a review.

use_cases.title: Refactor Existing Screens
use_cases.description: Use the skill to move legacy code toward strict TypeScript, validated forms, and project-approved component patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Feature Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quetrex-architect to review my feature plan for Quetrex. Check TDD steps, TypeScript types, App Router boundaries, Zod validation, and ShadCN UI usage.

prompt_templates.title: Guide Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quetrex-architect while I implement this Quetrex feature. Point out pattern violations and suggest the project-approved approach before I write each major part.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quetrex-architect to review these changed files. List violations with file and line references, explain the risk, and show the preferred Quetrex pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor for Compliance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quetrex-architect to create a refactor plan for this Quetrex module. Prioritize strict typing, validation, component boundaries, tests, and security fixes.

output_examples.input: Review a new dashboard feature before implementation.
output_examples.output: The response lists required tests, server component data loading, client component boundaries, validated API inputs, and approved ShadCN UI components.

output_examples.input: Audit a pull request that adds an API route and form.
output_examples.output: The response identifies missing Zod validation, incomplete form control usage, weak typing, and the required corrections with file references.

output_examples.input: Plan a refactor for a legacy Quetrex screen.
output_examples.output: The response provides a staged plan covering tests first, type cleanup, validated inputs, UI component replacement, and final coverage checks.

best_practices: Provide the relevant Quetrex files, feature goal, and expected user flow before asking for review.
Ask for file and line references when reviewing existing code.
Use the skill before implementation and again before submitting a pull request.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a general Next.js guide for projects without Quetrex conventions.
Do not skip local tests because the skill suggested a TDD path.
Do not accept generated examples without adapting names, schemas, and components to the real codebase.

faq.question: What projects is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for Quetrex projects that use strict TypeScript, Next.js App Router, ShadCN UI, Zod, and TDD.

faq.question: Can it run tests or modify files?
faq.answer: No. The skill declares Read, Grep, and Glob only, so it guides review and implementation without executing commands.

faq.question: Does it support pull request review?
faq.answer: Yes. It can list architecture violations, explain why they matter, and suggest the expected Quetrex pattern.

faq.question: Does it replace security review?
faq.answer: No. It highlights common issues such as missing validation, hardcoded secrets, and unsafe queries, but specialist review may still be needed.

faq.question: Why does it mention November 2025 standards?
faq.answer: The source skill states that its ShadCN UI guidance reflects the project standard updated for November 2025.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
